zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Hancock II於1550年5月1日出生在英國米德萊可倫敦時，他的父親Thomas Hancock30歲，母親Margaret Grubb-11歲。他於大約1581年與Sibill Coleman結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1626年4月18日在英國英格蘭希迪 本 倫敦享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: Hancock
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Hann + the hypocoristic suffix -cok, which was commonly added to personal names (see Cocke ).
Dutch: from Middle Dutch hanecoc ‘winkle, periwinkle’ (a type of shellfish), probably a metonymic occupational name for someone who gathered and sold shellfish.

History: Thomas Hancock, the uncle of Declaration of Independence signatory John Hancock (1736/7–93), was among the foremost of 18th-century American businessmen. He was a descendant of Nathaniel Hancock, who was known to have been in Cambridge, MA, as early as 1634. Born in Braintree, MA, John Hancock was president of the Second Continental Congress and the first governor of the state of MA.

variants.name: Hancox
meanings: English: variant of Hancock , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Handcock
meanings: English: variant of Hancock with intrusive -d-.

variants.name: Hann
meanings: German and Dutch: from a shortened pet form of the personal name Johann (see John ).

English: from the Middle English personal name Hann, commonly a pet form of Henry (Middle English Hanry). Hann could also be a rhyming pet form of Randolph (Middle English Randal, shortened to Ran or Rand). Han was also a pet form of Joh(a)n in Middle Dutch, and it is possible that this usage was brought to England by Flemings after the Norman Conquest, but no evidence has yet been found that it was similarly used in Middle English. See Henry and Randall , and compare Hankin , Hancock , and Hanks .
English: variant of Ann with prosthetic H-.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Hancock II was born on 1 May 1550, in London, Middlesex, England, his father, Thomas Hancock, was 30 and his mother, Margaret Grubb, was -11. He married Sibill Coleman about 1581. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 18 April 1626, in City of London, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 75, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

Il a épousé Sibill Coleman environ 1581. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Hancock II(이)가 1550년 5월 1일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Hancock(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Grubb(은)는 -11세였다. 그는 대략 1581년 Sibill Coleman(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1626년 4월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 시티 프 런던에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Hancock II nasceu em 1 de maio de 1550, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Hancock, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Grubb, tinha -11 anos. Ele casou-se com Sibill Coleman aproximadamente 1581. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 18 de abril de 1626, em City of London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Hancock II родился 1 мая 1550 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Hancock было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Grubb, -11. Он женился на женщине по имени Sibill Coleman около 1581 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 18 апреля 1626 г. в местности сити оф лондон, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Hancock II nació el 1 de mayo de 1550, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Hancock, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Margaret Grubb, tenía -11 años. Se casó con Sibill Coleman aproximadamente 1581. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 18 de abril de 1626, en City of London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
